Intel confirms rumored Core Ultra 9 290K Plus has been scrapped

By Aaron Klotz 26 March 26

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here's how it works.

Intel has confirmed it has scrapped the Core Ultra 9 290K Plus and will not release a special edition flagship for Arrow Lake refresh.

The decision leaves the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Core Ultra 5 250K Plus as the newest mainstream desktop launches.

Why it matters

Intel neglected to share its reasons for cancelling the 290K Plus, but it is easy to guess why.

Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter

Benchmark leaks suggested the chip would only offer a modest uplift over the 270K Plus.

Article continues below

Related reading

That would have made the part a difficult sell at flagship pricing, especially with Nova Lake approaching.

  • Arrow Lake refresh remains focused on smaller SKU updates.
